description: ADR-039 illustrative -- Movate fleet CAPACITY pane: queue depth + age via mdk.jobs.in_flight (per docs/observability.md, queue depth as a separate metric is NOT emitted today -- mdk.jobs.in_flight is the closest proxy + ADR 034 D3 confirms), worker saturation, pool stats across deployments (mdk.db.pool.size / .idle / .in_use / .waiting / .max), hot-tenants bubble (latency x volume). Tagged 'illustrative -- pending ADR 039 sign-off; assumes a customer template variable resolves either via Lighthouse subscription scope (Phase 1) or via the customer OTel attribute (Phase 2)'.

options.content:
### Capacity + saturation -- runbook

**What this pane answers:** which deployments are SATURATED? In-flight job depth, DB connection-pool occupancy, and a hot-tenants bubble that surfaces latency x volume together.

**Instruments used (all from `METRIC_NAMES`, verified against `docs/observability.md`):**
* `mdk.jobs.in_flight` -- up-down counter; ADR 034 D3 confirms this is the closest fleet proxy for queue depth today. A dedicated `mdk.queue.depth` is **deferred** per `src/movate/tracing/metrics.py` header (the seam between metrics and the StorageProvider deferred to a later item).
* `mdk.db.pool.size` / `.idle` / `.in_use` / `.waiting` / `.max` -- observable gauges, no attributes; per-pod identity comes from the OTel `Resource` (e.g. `service.instance.id`). Recorded by `register_pool_metrics()` (ADR 034 D3).
* Latency from `mdk.job.duration_ms` (ms histogram); volume from `mdk.jobs.completed`.

**Normal:** in-flight tracks the customer's expected concurrency; pool `.in_use` stays well under `.max` (target <80%); `.waiting` is ~0 (a non-zero waiting count means clients are queued for a DB connection -- the canary signal ADR 034 D3 watches for).

**If red:**
* **`mdk.db.pool.waiting` non-zero on a deployment for >5min** -- DB connection contention. ADR 034 D3's pool gauges are the SLO signal here; page the on-call for that customer's deployment.
* **`mdk.jobs.in_flight` flat-high while throughput drops** -- stuck worker(s); look for a hung tool call in the per-customer Jaeger / Langfuse drilldown.
* **One tenant dominates the hot-tenants bubble** (high latency AND high volume) -- not necessarily wrong (it's a heavy user); but if it forces another tenant into the high-latency-low-volume quadrant, the deployment may be under-sized.

**Cross-refs:** ADR 034 (data-plane scalability, the canonical place for pool semantics), ADR 015 (Langfuse drilldown), the per-customer `mdk-queue-and-pool.json` from PR #518 (single-deployment view of the same instruments).

panels.description: mdk.jobs.in_flight per (customer, deployment). Up-down counter -- the LIVE value, not a rate. Per docs/observability.md a dedicated mdk.queue.depth is deferred; in_flight is the fleet proxy ADR 034 D3 endorses.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.jobs.in_flight'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), depl = tostring(Properties['deployment'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize InFlight = max(ValueSum) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1m), cust, depl
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: In-flight jobs by (customer, deployment) -- queue depth proxy

panels.description: **Scaffold -- needs new instrument.** Per docs/observability.md a dedicated job-age / queue-age histogram is NOT emitted today (the queue.depth note in src/movate/tracing/metrics.py header defers it). Promote to a Markdown banner; the real signal is upstream in mdk.jobs.in_flight + mdk.job.duration_ms.

options.content:
### Queue AGE (scaffold) -- needs new instrument

**Needs new instrument:** `mdk.queue.age_ms` (histogram, attributes: `customer`, `deployment`).

NOT emitted today. Per `src/movate/tracing/metrics.py` header, the StorageProvider count/age queries are deferred to keep the metrics module off the storage seam.

Until it lands, queue-age proxy = `mdk.job.duration_ms` p99 minus the in-runtime processing time -- but the in-runtime time isn't separately instrumented either, so this is genuinely a gap. **See ADR 039 Open Questions** (which is the canonical place to track new instruments needed for the fleet view) and `src/movate/tracing/metrics.py` header for the deferral rationale.

panels.description: Pool occupancy ratio = mdk.db.pool.in_use / mdk.db.pool.max per pod, aggregated per (customer, deployment). Per docs/observability.md these gauges carry NO attributes -- per-pod identity comes from the OTel Resource (service.instance.id) on the AppMetrics row.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let inuse =
  AppMetrics
  | where Name == 'mdk.db.pool.in_use'
  | extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), depl = tostring(Properties['deployment'])
  | where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
  | summarize InUse = avg(ValueSum) by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m), cust, depl;
let maxp =
  AppMetrics
  | where Name == 'mdk.db.pool.max'
  | extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), depl = tostring(Properties['deployment'])
  | where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
  | summarize Max = avg(ValueSum) by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m), cust, depl;
inuse
| join kind=inner maxp on TimeGenerated, cust, depl
| extend Occupancy = todouble(InUse) / todouble(iff(Max > 0, Max, 1))
| project TimeGenerated, cust, depl, Occupancy
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Pool occupancy (mdk.db.pool.in_use / mdk.db.pool.max)

panels.description: mdk.db.pool.waiting per (customer, deployment). Non-zero means clients are queued for a DB connection -- the canary signal ADR 034 D3 watches for. A SUSTAINED non-zero is the page-out condition.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.db.pool.waiting'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), depl = tostring(Properties['deployment'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize Waiting = max(ValueSum) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1m), cust, depl
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Pool waiting (mdk.db.pool.waiting) -- SLO canary per ADR 034 D3

panels.description: Pool size + idle snapshot across the fleet -- a flat table reading the current values of mdk.db.pool.size and mdk.db.pool.idle per (customer, deployment).
panels.title: Pool snapshot (size / idle / in_use / max)

panels.description: Bubble per tenant: x = run volume from mdk.jobs.completed, y = p95 latency from mdk.job.duration_ms. Top-right corner = high-volume + slow tenants (the ones at risk of breaching SLO from sheer pressure).

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let vol =
  AppMetrics
  | where Name == 'mdk.jobs.completed'
  | extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer'])
  | where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
  | summarize Volume = sum(ValueSum) by cust;
let lat =
  AppMetrics
  | where Name == 'mdk.job.duration_ms'
  | extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer'])
  | where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
  | summarize P95 = percentile(ValueSum / ValueCount, 95) by cust;
vol
| join kind=inner lat on cust
| project Tenant = cust, Volume, P95
| order by Volume desc

panels.title: Hot tenants -- volume vs p95 latency

list.definition: Phase 1: Lighthouse subscription scope. Phase 2: hashed customer OTel resource attribute (ADR 039 D4).
list.label: customer
list.name: customer

title: Movate fleet -- capacity + saturation (illustrative, ADR 039)
